whatItMeasures: Creatinine is a waste product generated by normal muscle metabolism that is filtered out of the blood by the kidneys and excreted in urine; measuring it in the blood is a standard way to assess how well the kidneys are filtering waste.
causesOfHigh: Chronic kidney disease (most common cause of persistently elevated creatinine)
Acute kidney injury (dehydration, medications, infections, obstruction)
High dietary meat or creatine supplement intake
Large muscle mass or intense exercise
Certain medications that block creatinine secretion (trimethoprim, cimetidine)
causesOfHighSource: NHLBI. Chronic Kidney Disease. https://www.nhlbi.nih.gov/health/kidney-disease; NHS. Chronic kidney disease. https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/kidney-disease/
causesOfLow: Low muscle mass (elderly individuals, prolonged bed rest, muscle-wasting conditions)
Pregnancy (increased blood volume and kidney filtration rate lower creatinine)

high: Often no symptoms in early kidney disease
Swelling (oedema) of the ankles, feet, or face
Decreased urine output or changes in urine frequency
low: Low creatinine generally causes no direct symptoms

citations.title: Chronic Kidney Disease
citations.source: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NHLBI)
